description_long: This mosque features a large central dome flanked by two smaller domes, all with smooth, rounded surfaces and a pale, stone-like exterior. Two tall, slender minarets with pointed finials stand symmetrically at the front corners, each adorned with subtle horizontal banding and geometric bases. The main entrance is centrally located, marked by a recessed archway and a slightly protruding facade. The structure is set on a broad, rectangular platform with a stepped base, and the overall design is minimalist, lacking intricate surface ornamentation. The color palette is primarily off-white and beige, giving the mosque a serene and monumental appearance. The architectural style suggests influences from Mughal and Indo-Islamic traditions, with its domes, minarets, and rectilinear layout.
description: A pale, stone-like mosque with a large central dome, two smaller domes, and two tall minarets at the front corners, set on a broad rectangular base. The design is minimalist and symmetrical, with a central entrance and subtle Mughal/Indo-Islamic influences.
description_view_0: Front view showing the main entrance, large central dome, two smaller domes, and both minarets symmetrically positioned at the front corners.
description_view_1: Left side view highlighting the left minaret, one small dome, and the side profile of the central dome and entrance.
description_view_2: Rear view displaying the back of the central dome, the platform, and the symmetrical arrangement of the domes and minarets.
description_view_3: Right side view focusing on the right minaret, one small dome, and the side profile of the central dome and entrance.
attribute_dome: Three domes are visible: one large central dome and two smaller domes at the front corners, all smooth, rounded, and off-white with no visible decorative features.
attribute_minaret: Two tall, slender minarets with pointed finials, positioned symmetrically at the front corners, rising significantly above the main structure and featuring subtle horizontal banding.
attribute_architectural_style: Mughal/Indo-Islamic, characterized by large domes, tall minarets, and a rectilinear, symmetrical layout with minimal ornamentation.
